conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: For deployment, let's use Kubernetes. It's the standard for container orchestration.

conversation.speaker: assistant
conversation.text: Kubernetes gives us auto-scaling and self-healing. I'll set up the clusters.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: Kubernetes is too complex for our needs. Let's use Docker Compose. It's simpler for our single-node setup.

conversation.speaker: assistant
conversation.text: Docker Compose is much easier to manage. I'll create the compose file.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: We need high availability. Docker Swarm is simpler than Kubernetes and provides HA. Let's use Swarm.

conversation.speaker: assistant
conversation.text: Swarm is a good middle ground. I'll set up the swarm cluster.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: Actually, our cloud provider has managed Kubernetes. EKS simplifies everything. Let's use EKS.

conversation.speaker: assistant
conversation.text: EKS is well-supported. I'll migrate to managed Kubernetes.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: We just realized our workload is stateful. EKS with stateful sets is complicated. Let's use Nomad. It handles stateful workloads better.

conversation.speaker: assistant
conversation.text: Nomad is a HashiCorp tool. I'll learn it.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: No, this is too much. Let's stick with Kubernetes. It's the most transferable skill and we can always simplify later. Use EKS.

query: What orchestration platform is the team using for the production deployment?
